examples.title: Algorithm: K-Means Clustering
examples.description: A centroid-based clustering algorithm that partitions data into k clusters.
examples.code:
ALGORITHM K-Means
INPUT: dataset, k (number of clusters)
OUTPUT: cluster assignments, centroids

BEGIN
 1. Initialize k centroids randomly
 2. Assign each data point to the closest centroid
 3. Update centroids as the mean of all data points assigned to each cluster
 4. Repeat steps 2-3 until convergence
END
examples.explanation: K-Means is a simple and efficient clustering algorithm, but it requires the number of clusters (k) to be specified beforehand and is sensitive to initial centroid selection.

examples.title: Python: K-Means Clustering
examples.description: Implementation of the K-Means clustering algorithm in Python.
examples.code:
import numpy as np
from sklearn.cluster import KMeans

# Generate sample data
np.random.seed(0)
data = np.random.rand(100, 2)

# Create a KMeans instance with k=3 clusters
kmeans = KMeans(n_clusters=3)

# Fit the model to the data
kmeans.fit(data)

# Get the cluster assignments and centroids
labels = kmeans.labels_
centroids = kmeans.cluster_centers_

print(labels)
print(centroids)
examples.explanation: This code demonstrates how to use the scikit-learn library to perform K-Means clustering in Python.

examples.title: Python: DBSCAN Clustering
examples.description: Implementation of the DBSCAN clustering algorithm in Python.
examples.code:
import numpy as np
from sklearn.cluster import DBSCAN

# Generate sample data
np.random.seed(0)
data = np.random.rand(100, 2)

# Create a DBSCAN instance with epsilon=0.5 and min_samples=10
 dbscan = DBSCAN(eps=0.5, min_samples=10)

# Fit the model to the data
 dbscan.fit(data)

# Get the cluster assignments
labels = dbscan.labels_

print(labels)
examples.explanation: This code demonstrates how to use the scikit-learn library to perform DBSCAN clustering in Python.
